service identification:
Service
TAM JBaaS for Argenta
Unique ID
PRD.0.L.145
Service Model
Technical Application Management (TAM)
This service is dependant of other layers (IaaS and/or PaaS) which are indicated in the ‘Obligatory’ chapter of this document and which have their own Service Description. The combination of the layers assures the customer of the ‘infrastructure up to the technical application’ management. These obligatority services are called the ‘Platform’.
technical application management:
The Service provides the tasks and activities needed to manage the JBoss application as a Service (JBoss as a Service – JbaaS) (The Application) from a technical perspective. Together with the underlaying obligatory Platform services, Cegeka unburdens the customer with all technical aspects from infrastructure up to the technical Application.
JBoss as a Service (JBaaS) is a cloud computing service model that provides users with an access to a JBoss instance without the need for setting up physical hardware, installing software or configuring the platform according to best practice.
The solution allows you to “grow-as-you-go” and is composed of pre-defined service packages. The services included in these packages, turn this solution into a genuine “as a service” offering.  Combining these managed services with licensing for the CPU cores you actually use, is gives the Customer the advantage to run their application landscape in a cost efficient way.
The JBaaS service offers the following benefits to customers:
The Standard Service features are focussed to keep the Service up-and-running, to keep it secure and to optimize its technical parameters.
The Customer is responsible for the setup and management of the containers running within the JBoss environments.
the application: JBoss application server is an open-source platform, developed by RedHat, used for implementing Java applications and a wide variety of other software applications. You can build and deploy Java services to be scaled to fit the size of your business.
standard service features:
Cegeka ensures skilled engineers with experience on the RedHat JBoss technology in order to perform the Service.
Cegeka advises in the setup of the underlaying platform in order to set it up according to the technology requirements of the Application. This may be different for each Customer environment (DTAP - Development, Test, Acceptance, Production and it’s variants)..
High availability setup
this setup advise includes also the setup of HA (High Available) environments where needed. Cegeka will advise a cost effective solution within the context  of the available platform(s) of the Customer and the best practices of the Application.
JBaaS management flavors
Cegeka has developed the JBaaS solution in 3 flavours: Bronze, Silver and Gold. The services layer on top of these technical building blocks is uniform and transparent to the customer and end user.
Every flavour comes with a minimum availability of the entire JBoss platform related to the underlying architecture.
Bronze: One or more JBoss instances in one datacenter – no DR enabled
Silver: One or more (in case HA is needed for that application on that environment) JBoss instances in one datacenter, with DR to the other datacenter, using the container platform mechanism. The necessary capacity is reserved in the other datacenter.
This is primary intended for non-PRD environments, since the RTO is lower.
RTO: 24 hours, RPO: 0 minutes (no data loss)
Gold: Two or more JBoss instances (running the same applications – for HA purposes) in one datacenter, with DR to the other datacenter, using the container platform mechanism. The necessary capacity is reserved in the other datacenter.
This setup is primary intended for PRD environments.
RTO: 2 hours, RPO: 0 minutes (no data loss)

Performance management
JBoss performance management is the process of monitoring, analyzing and performing the subsequent performance tweaks of the JBoss environment in order to optimize performance and increase efficiency.
Global
Alerts indicating performance issues are using the standard notification process within the incident management procedure and service.
STD
STD
STD
Burst
Bursting for peak workload times: the possibility to burst to a higher performance level for a limited time.
REQ
STD
STD
Trend analysis
Pro-active services where we apply trend analysis to acquired information so that we can compare the number of events in a specific timeframe with the number of events in other time periods. Historical data collection mechanisms and the ability to translate the data into reports that show the changes affecting JBoss performance over time.
Findings are presented as per the agreed governance.

REQ

Capacity management
Capacity planning acknowledges that the business requirements on the system may increase, and forecasts how much resources must be added to the JBaaS environment to ensure that the user experience continues uninterrupted.
Global
Alerts are integrated with the standard incident and problem management tooling and governance services.
A capacity plan, based on known communicated customer projects will be documented and taken into account.

Monitoring
Cegeka monitoring and alerting is a service used to monitor devices and JBoss instances on various parameters. Alerts are integrated with the standard incident and problem management tooling and governance services.
Base (Capacity, Availability)
Real-time monitoring of availability, capacity, connectivity, process and security parameters.

Application Performance
Application Performance Monitoring (APM) is a service, which monitors the end-to-end performance of application interactions with users and application components.

Logging
The logging service collects, stores and retains log information in a log repository. The log repository ensures that generated logs are collected, securely retained according to regulation and are available from a single point of access.

Sizes and units
The size of the JBoss instances is constrained by the underlaying platform.
Total instance cost consists of:
Cegeka performs and maintains the technical installation of the Application on the agreed platform ensuring its correct working.
Technical setup is documented on operational level and is available to the Customer.
Cegeka is responsible for the technical operation of the Application and to assure it’s correct running during the agreed availability windows.
Recurrent maintenance activities (housekeeping) are needed to ensure the correct working of the Application. Automation is applied where possible.
Proactive management
Housekeeping includes following regular checks:
Resolution actions are triggered when needed and followed to assure the SLA.
Patching
The service includes a maximum of 4 patch cycles (per environment) per year for the managed Application (inclusive the system database(s) if applicable). Patching is done in accordance to the operational Patch Plan.
Notes:
Additional patching can be done on RFC basis.
Patching of the underlying platform is part of the Service of that platform.
Life Cycle Management
Cegeka monitors the Technology version of the service and notifies the Customer in accordance with the agreed LCM policy (see cross-functional Services) when used versions are subject to end-of-support or end-of-life. Appropriate actions (updates, replacement or other actions) are planned in accordance with agreed maintenance windows and will be executed on T&M basis in case of major updates. Patches and minor updates are included in the service cost as part of ‘Patching’.
No specific monitoring for the Application is needed. Containers are auto-restarted by Openshift.
Monitoring improvements will be done during the lifecycle of the Service and are based on evaluation of incidents, problem management or other suggestions.
The underlaying platform is also monitored according to the related Service Description of the platform.
Monitoring events are evaluated during the Service Window in accordance to the Service Level of the individual environment. Notable events are treated as Incident tickets and are handled according to regular incident management.
Management of technical users
The management of technical users (for engineers) is done by Cegeka in agreement with the Customer and in accordance to the Customer IAM policies.
JBoss has 6 main roles: enduser, application component provider, application assembler, deployer, image assembler and system administrator. By default, Cegeka assumes the last 2 roles. The customer typically assumes the first 4 roles. Each of these roles provides a set of functionality.
Detection of security incidents
The security management is performed following the Cegeka internal security policies and the contractual Customer security policies.
Security incidents are created in case a security breach is detected and is immediately communicated to the Customer and Cegeka stakeholders.
SIEM
Additional monitoring can be done based on System and/or Application logs, which can be forwarded to a Customer SIEM solution (Security reason) or other solution (compliance reason, e.g. central collection of application logs to oversee the application stack) on request.
Underlaying Platform hardening
The underlaying platform and OS will be hardened as part of and according to the Service Description of the related Services.
The operational Backup Plan contains the information on frequency and retention of the backups in accordance to the Customer backup policy.
Restores can be done both on file and on full system level. These can be requested via the regular RFC process.
Duration of a restore is dependant of the request (file or full; size of the item(s) to be restored).
Application settings are subject to the backup of the underlaying platform.
Cegeka ensures the backup of the technical configuration data (Openshift platform) The base image is contained in the Nexus repository.
Besides monitoring incidents (see ‘Monitoring’) also other incidents can be reported by both the Customer and Cegeka. These will be subject to the regular incident management process (See Cross-functionals).
Related to incident management, Cegeka performs also problem management (See Cross-functionals).
The functional setup and monitoring of the Application is the responsibility of the Customer.

data classification:
Cegeka requires no access to confidential and/or personal data to perform this Service.
Engineer credentials do not need to access such data for the purpose of this Service.
In case of assistance in investigations of functional issues, Cegeka personnel may see or receive fragments of such data in case it is shared by the Customer (data fragments in the sense of exports, screen sharing, screen shots, etc. for investigation purposes).

adherence to cross-functional services:
The Service described in this Service Description adheres to the Cross-functional Services as described in  the contract where this Service is residing unless documented in the table below:
Cross-Functional Service
Deviation from adherence to cross-functional services
Anti-Malware management
This service is not subject to Anti-Malware management.
Anti-Malware management is done on the underlaying OS management layer.
Vulnerability management & compliancy management (hardening)
This service is not subject to vulnerability & compliancy management.
Vulnerability & compliancy management is done on the underlaying OS management layer.
The Cross -Functional services rely on the Cegeka standard framework for processes and tooling.
